value.found_quote: MCP servers communicate with your agent using different transport protocols... If the docs give you a command to run (like `npx @modelcontextprotocol/server-github`), use stdio... If the docs give you a URL, use HTTP or SSE... If you're building your own tools in code, use an SDK MCP server
value.rationale: The page explicitly documents three transport types for MCP servers: (1) stdio — local processes communicating via stdin/stdout, (2) HTTP/SSE — for cloud-hosted MCP servers and remote APIs, and (3) SDK MCP servers — custom tools defined directly in application code (SDK-embedded). This directly substantiates the claim that MCP servers can communicate via stdio, HTTP/SSE, or SDK-embedded transports, supporting local processes and cloud-hosted or remote APIs. The page presents these as currently available features with working code examples, indicating active status.
